reasoning_content: We need to determine if 359 is a prime number. A prime number is a natural number greater than 1 that has no positive divisors other than 1 and itself. Check if 359 is divisible by any small primes. Let's test: 2: 359 is odd, no. 3: sum of digits 3+5+9=17, not divisible by 3. 5: last digit not 0 or 5. 7: 7*51=357, remainder 2, so not divisible. 11: 11*32=352, remainder 7, not divisible. 13: 13*27=351, remainder 8, not divisible. 17: 17*21=357, remainder 2, not divisible. 19: 19*18=342, remainder 17, not divisible. 23: 23*15=345, remainder 14, not divisible. 29: 29*12=348, remainder 11, not divisible. 31: 31*11=341, remainder 18, not divisible. 37: 37*9=333, remainder 26, not divisible. 41: 41*8=328, remainder 31, not divisible. 43: 43*8=344, remainder 15, not divisible. 47: 47*7=329, remainder 30, not divisible. 53: 53*6=318, remainder 41, not divisible. Continue up to sqrt(359) which is about 18.95, so we only need to check primes up to 17? Actually sqrt is about 18.95, so primes up to 19? 19 we already checked. Actually we should check up to 19, but we've done up to 19. Also 17, 19. So no divisors found. Thus 359 is prime. Answer yes.
